commands.review_summary:
The PR changes MiniMax and Alibaba Coding Plan endpoint override normalization to reject explicit non-HTTPS schemes and adds regression tests for accepted HTTPS/bare-host values and rejected non-HTTPS values.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main accepts explicit non-HTTPS MiniMax and Alibaba override URLs, and the fetchers attach cookies or bearer/API-key credentials to resolved URLs. I did not run live provider probes per AGENTS.md because this can involve real account or Keychain access.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-provided real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance or revision of the invalid-override fail-closed behavior, so it is not a safe automated repair lane now.

target.title: [security] fix(providers): reject insecure credentialed endpoint overrides
target.body:
## Summary

This PR hardens credentialed provider usage requests so endpoint overrides cannot downgrade token-bearing traffic to non-HTTPS URLs.

- Rejects explicit non-HTTPS MiniMax usage endpoint overrides before cookies are attached.
- Rejects explicit non-HTTPS Alibaba Coding Plan quota/host overrides before bearer tokens are attached.
- Preserves the existing bare-host convenience by continuing to normalize bare values to `https://...`.
- Adds regression coverage for HTTPS/bare-host acceptance and `http://` / non-HTTPS rejection.

## Security issues covered

| Issue | Impact | Severity |
|-------|--------|----------|
| Insecure credentialed endpoint overrides for MiniMax and Alibaba Coding Plan usage requests | Local launch/configuration injection or an unsafe wrapper could cause provider cookies or bearer tokens to be sent to cleartext or unsupported schemes. | Medium |

## Before this PR

- MiniMax and Alibaba Coding Plan endpoint override settings accepted explicit URL schemes such as `http://...`.
- The usage fetchers still attached provider credentials to requests built from those override values.
- Tests covered override parsing behavior, but did not lock in a credentialed-transport boundary for non-HTTPS explicit URLs.

## After this PR

- Explicit override URLs must use HTTPS before they are used for credentialed provider requests.
- Bare host/path overrides remain supported and are still interpreted as HTTPS for compatibility.
- Regression tests cover accepted HTTPS/bare values and rejected `http://` / non-HTTPS values for the affected providers.

## Why this matters

Provider usage requests carry credentials that CodexBar uses on the user's behalf:

- MiniMax usage requests attach provider cookies.
- Alibaba Coding Plan usage requests attach bearer-token credentials.

Endpoint overrides are useful for development and compatible deployments, but token-bearing requests should not silently follow a cleartext or unsupported-scheme override. Enforcing HTTPS at the override boundary keeps the existing feature while preventing accidental credential disclosure through unsafe launch/configuration state.

## Attack flow

```text
Attacker-controlled or unsafe local launch/configuration environment
    -> MiniMax / Alibaba Coding Plan endpoint override
        -> explicit non-HTTPS URL accepted
            -> credentialed usage fetcher attaches cookies or bearer token
                -> credential exposure over cleartext or unsupported endpoint
```

## Affected code

| Issue | Files |
|-------|-------|
| MiniMax credentialed endpoint override accepts explicit non-HTTPS URLs | `Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/MiniMax/MiniMaxSettingsReader.swift`, `Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/MiniMax/MiniMaxUsageFetcher.swift`, `Tests/CodexBarTests/MiniMaxProviderTests.swift` |
| Alibaba Coding Plan credentialed endpoint override accepts explicit non-HTTPS URLs | `Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/Alibaba/AlibabaCodingPlanSettingsReader.swift`, `Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/Alibaba/AlibabaCodingPlanUsageFetcher.swift`, `Tests/CodexBarTests/AlibabaCodingPlanProviderTests.swift` |

## Root cause

MiniMax credentialed endpoint overrides:
- Explicit override URLs were normalized without rejecting non-HTTPS schemes.
- The credentialed request path trusted the resulting URL/host before attaching cookies.

Alibaba Coding Plan credentialed endpoint overrides:
- Explicit quota URL and host overrides were accepted without requiring HTTPS.
- The usage fetcher could compose credentialed requests from an override value before enforcing the transport boundary.

## CVSS assessment

| Issue | CVSS v3.1 | Vector |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| Insecure credentialed provider endpoint overrides | 5.0 Medium | `CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N` |

Rationale:
- This is a local/configuration-assisted credential-disclosure issue, not unauthenticated remote compromise.
- The impact is confidentiality of provider credentials attached to usage requests.
- Severity can vary by deployment depending on who controls the process environment or wrapper that launches CodexBar.

## Safe reproduction steps

### 1. MiniMax override downgrade

1. Configure a MiniMax usage endpoint override with an explicit non-HTTPS value such as `http://example.test/usage`.
2. Exercise the MiniMax usage settings/fetcher path.
3. Before this PR, the override is accepted and can be used to build a token-bearing request.
4. After this PR, the override is rejected before cookies are attached.

### 2. Alibaba Coding Plan override downgrade

1. Configure an Alibaba Coding Plan quota URL or host override with an explicit non-HTTPS value such as `http://example.test`.
2. Exercise the Alibaba Coding Plan usage settings/fetcher path.
3. Before this PR, the override is accepted and can be used to build a bearer-token request.
4. After this PR, the override is rejected before bearer credentials are attached.

## Expected vulnerable behavior

- Explicit non-HTTPS endpoint overrides should not be accepted for credentialed provider requests.
- Pre-patch behavior accepted those override values for the affected usage providers.
- The safe proof signal is the settings/fetcher helper accepting or rejecting the override value; no real provider secrets or production endpoints are needed.

## Changes in this PR

- Reject explicit non-HTTPS URL overrides in `MiniMaxSettingsReader`.
- Reject non-HTTPS MiniMax host overrides before composing credentialed usage URLs.
- Reject explicit non-HTTPS quota URL overrides in `AlibabaCodingPlanSettingsReader`.
- Reject non-HTTPS Alibaba Coding Plan host overrides in the settings/fetcher helpers.
- Preserve bare-host compatibility by continuing to treat bare override values as HTTPS.
- Add regression tests for accepted HTTPS/bare override values and rejected explicit non-HTTPS values.

## Files changed

| Category | Files | What changed |
|----------|-------|--------------|
| MiniMax provider hardening | `Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/MiniMax/MiniMaxSettingsReader.swift`, `Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/MiniMax/MiniMaxUsageFetcher.swift` | Enforces HTTPS for explicit credentialed endpoint overrides while preserving bare-host HTTPS normalization. |
| Alibaba provider hardening | `Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/Alibaba/AlibabaCodingPlanSettingsReader.swift`, `Sources/CodexBarCore/Providers/Alibaba/AlibabaCodingPlanUsageFetcher.swift` | Enforces HTTPS for quota/host overrides before bearer-token usage requests are composed. |
| Regression tests | `Tests/CodexBarTests/MiniMaxProviderTests.swift`, `Tests/CodexBarTests/AlibabaCodingPlanProviderTests.swift` | Adds acceptance/rejection coverage for HTTPS, bare-host, and explicit non-HTTPS overrides. |

## Maintainer impact

- The patch is limited to MiniMax and Alibaba Coding Plan credentialed usage endpoint override handling plus tests.
- Existing HTTPS override values continue to work.
- Existing bare-host override values continue to work by being interpreted as HTTPS.
- Non-HTTPS explicit overrides now fail closed for credentialed usage requests.
- No unrelated frontend, storage, or provider flows are changed.

## Fix rationale

- The HTTPS check belongs at the override normalization/composition boundary, before provider credentials are attached.
- The change is narrow: it does not remove endpoint overrides or change default provider endpoints.
- Regression tests cover both compatibility-preserving behavior and the rejected downgrade cases.

## Type of change

- [x] Security fix
- [x] Tests
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Refactor with no behavior change

## Test plan

- [x] Build the affected core target.
- [x] Add targeted regression coverage for MiniMax override handling.
- [x] Add targeted regression coverage for Alibaba Coding Plan override handling.
- [ ] Complete the targeted test command in this local checkout; it is currently blocked by an unrelated transitive macOS app dependency build issue described below.

Executed with:

- `swift build --target CodexBarCore`
- `swift test --filter 'MiniMaxSettingsReaderTests|AlibabaCodingPlanSettingsReaderTests'`

Local result:

- `swift build --target CodexBarCore` completed successfully.
- `swift test --filter 'MiniMaxSettingsReaderTests|AlibabaCodingPlanSettingsReaderTests'` did not complete in this checkout because the `CodexBarTests` target builds the macOS app dependency graph and fails in the transitive `KeyboardShortcuts` checkout with `PreviewsMacros` not found for SwiftUI `#Preview` macros. The new assertions are included as regression coverage for normal CI/test environments.

## Disclosure notes

- This PR is scoped to credentialed endpoint override handling for MiniMax and Alibaba Coding Plan usage requests.
- No real provider credentials, production endpoints, or destructive actions are needed to reproduce the issue.
- This is not framed as unauthenticated remote code execution; the attacker condition is control over local launch/configuration state or an unsafe wrapper around CodexBar.
- No unrelated files are intentionally changed.
